defends: Vercel project observability and the edge firewall: the deployed app emits telemetry (attacks/anomalies are visible) and has the firewall standing in front of it for first-line detection and blocking (T1190 exploit public-facing application).

state_document.observability_enabled: Vercel project: Observability is enabled (telemetry/monitoring on)
state_document.firewall_enabled: Vercel project: the Firewall is enabled (edge protection on)

live_target.source: Vercel project settings via the Vercel API (project observability + firewall configuration)
live_target.note: unknown without live creds; use --state-fixture offline

self_guard: bad fixture sets firewall_enabled=false (disabled control injected); validator must flag fail (negative control fires). good fixture has both keys true and must pass clean.
note: Fixed evidence manifest — the audited project surface and the required state keys are not model discretion (WORKING_METHOD §1). All required keys must be true to pass; any false fails; any missing/unreadable is unknown.
